from pyspark.sql import functions as F
from pyspark.sql.window import Window
from delta.tables import DeltaTable

PROD_PATH = "abfss://dl-juror-eric-voters-temp@baubaisadfsaprod.dfs.core.windows.net/voters_deduplicated_delta"
STG_PATH  = "abfss://dl-juror-eric-voters-temp@baubaisadfsastg.dfs.core.windows.net/voters_deduplicated_delta"

POSTCODE_START_PATTERN = r"^([A-Z]{1,2}[0-9][A-Z0-9]?)"

# Load both tables
prod_df = spark.read.format("delta").load(PROD_PATH)
stg_df  = spark.read.format("delta").load(STG_PATH)

# Derive the postcode prefix on both sides
prod_df = prod_df.withColumn(
    "pc_start",
    F.regexp_extract(F.upper(F.trim(F.col("zip"))), POSTCODE_START_PATTERN, 1)
)
stg_df = stg_df.withColumn(
    "pc_start",
    F.regexp_extract(F.upper(F.trim(F.col("zip"))), POSTCODE_START_PATTERN, 1)
)

# Lookup 1: full postcode -> most common rec_num
zip_lookup = (
    prod_df
    .filter(F.col("zip").isNotNull() & F.col("rec_num").isNotNull())
    .groupBy("zip", "rec_num").count()
    .withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(
        Window.partitionBy("zip").orderBy(F.desc("count"))))
    .filter("rn = 1")
    .select("zip", F.col("rec_num").alias("prod_rec_num"))
)

# Lookup 2: postcode prefix -> most common rec_num (fallback)
prefix_lookup = (
    prod_df
    .filter((F.col("pc_start") != "") & F.col("rec_num").isNotNull())
    .groupBy("pc_start", "rec_num").count()
    .withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(
        Window.partitionBy("pc_start").orderBy(F.desc("count"))))
    .filter("rn = 1")
    .select("pc_start", F.col("rec_num").alias("prefix_rec_num"))
)

# Corrections set
corrections = (
    stg_df.select("hash_id", "zip", "pc_start", "rec_num")
    .join(zip_lookup, "zip", "left")
    .join(prefix_lookup, "pc_start", "left")
    .withColumn("new_rec_num", F.coalesce("prod_rec_num", "prefix_rec_num"))
    .filter(
        F.col("new_rec_num").isNotNull() &
        (
            (F.col("new_rec_num") != F.col("rec_num")) |
            F.col("rec_num").isNull()
        )
    )
    .select("hash_id", "new_rec_num", "pc_start")
)

print(f"Rows to correct: {corrections.count():,}")
corrections.show(20, truncate=False)

stg_delta = DeltaTable.forPath(spark, STG_PATH)

update_set = {
    "rec_num": "s.new_rec_num",
    "postcode_start": "s.pc_start"
}

# Only sync the audit column if it actually exists
if "last_updated" in stg_df.columns:
    update_set["last_updated"] = "current_timestamp()"

(stg_delta.alias("t")
    .merge(corrections.alias("s"), "t.hash_id = s.hash_id")
    .whenMatchedUpdate(set=update_set)
    .execute())
